rln_injury:
12/10/2024
Per referring MD: 72 y.o. female with a history of ACDF and thyroidectomy (2000) c/b left vocal cord paralysis (resolved), long standing Left Bell's palsy (longstanding), OSA, GERD, and HCC currently on chemo who reports an episode of dyspnea (isolated episode of laryngospasm). Specifically, they reports remembering clearing her throat while in a twilight sleep and suddenly awakening unable to inhale, exhale, cough, or speak. This lasted for a few seconds. It has not happened again, and has only happened once before while in the ICU after her ACDF back in 2000. She denies syncope with this episode, and it self resolved spontaneously. She did not cough anything up after its resolution. She does have a history of gastroparesis and has been on Nexium chronically for reflux. Flexible fiberoptic laryngoscopy demonstrates mild cobblestoning and posterior laryngeal erythema suggestive of reflux.
